title: Triple chocolate brownies
subtitle: Dense, fudgy brownies with dark, milk, and white chocolate
excerpt: Deeply fudgy brownies built on a dark chocolate base with milk and white chocolate chips folded through. Dense, with crackled tops and edges that set firmer than the centre.
sourceNotes: The American brownie in its fudgy form (distinguished from the cake-like brownie by a lower flour ratio) is documented from American cookbooks of the early twentieth century. The fudgy brownie became the definitive British bakery brownie in the 1990s through American-influenced cafes. This recipe uses the high chocolate, low flour ratio that produces the characteristic dense, barely-set interior, synthesised from methods in Alice Medrich's Bittersweet (2003) and Hummingbird Bakery Cookbook (Tim Malouf, 2009).

recipe.freezeNotes: Cool completely. Cut into portions and freeze in a sealed bag for up to 3 months. Thaw at room temperature.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Brownies improve after 12 to 24 hours as the texture sets. Bake the day before serving for the best result.

content.text: Fudgy brownies are underdone by cake standards. A skewer will not come out clean; it should come out with moist crumbs clinging to it. The surface should have crackled and set, and the edges should be just pulling away from the sides of the tin. The centre will firm as it cools.

Do not cut the brownies while they are still warm. They will compress and crumble under the knife. Leave in the tin until completely cool (at least 2 hours), then refrigerate for 30 minutes before cutting. A warm knife held under hot water and dried makes cleaner cuts.

Heat the oven to 180°C fan. Grease and line a 20 x 20 cm square tin. Melt the {{dark-chocolate}} and {{unsalted-butter}} together in a heatproof bowl set over a pan of barely simmering water, stirring until smooth. Remove from the heat and stir in the {{caster-sugar}}. Leave to cool for 5 minutes. Beat in the {{eggs}} one at a time, then the {{vanilla-extract}}. Fold in the {{plain-flour}}, {{cocoa-powder}}, and {{fine-sea-salt}} until just combined. Fold in the {{chocolate-chips}}.

Pour the batter into the tin and spread level. Bake for 22 to 26 minutes until the surface has crackled and set and a skewer inserted 2 cm from the edge comes out clean while one inserted in the centre comes out with moist crumbs. The centre should have a slight wobble. Cool completely in the tin (at least 2 hours) before cutting into 16 squares.
